joke:
Developer: Yaar, mera code local pe bilkul mast chal raha hai!
Senior: Toh ek kaam kar, client ko tera computer hi gift kar dete hain.

Programming seekhne ke stages:
1. Print('Hello World') - I am GOD!
2. Syntax Error - Huh, easy fixing.
3. Logic Error - Hmm...
4. Seg Fault - *Starts looking for new career*
CSS seekhna aasan hai, bas subah 5 baje uth ke ek button ko center karne ki koshish karo, sab normal ho jayega.

Beta: Papa, log shaadi kyun karte hain?
Developer Papa: Kyunki jab team badhti hai, tabhi toh bugs (problems) handle hote hain!
Code likhte waqt 90% time sirf ye sochne me nikal jata hai ki is variable ka naam kya rakhu taaki doosra coder mujhe gaali na de.

Artificial Intelligence: Ek din main pure world pe control karunga.
Windows Update: Pehle mera system restart hone de, phir sochio.

Database Administrator: Mujhe ek naya ghar chahiye.
Agent: SQL format me chahiye ya NoSQL?
DBA: SQL! Partitioning to automatic honi chahiye.
Aache coders wo nahi hote jo code jaldi likhte hain. Aache coders wo hote hain jo stackoverflow pe bina post kiye solution dhoond lete hain.

Senior Coder: Ye kisne likha hai ganda code?
Junior: Maine, 6 mahine pehle sir.
Senior: Oh, actually structure bahut solid lag raha hai, smart logic!

Programmer's Diet:
BreakFast: Coffee + Bugs
Lunch: Cold Coffee + More Bugs
Dinner: Code Refactoring
Snacks: Tears and regret.

Python: Mujhe seekhna bahut aasan hai.
C++: Mujhe seekh ke dikhao toh maane.
HTML: Guyz, main bhi ek language hoon na? 🥺
Java/C++: Tum chup raho aur tag close karo.

Git commit messages over time:
1. 'Initial commit'
2. 'Added some features'
3. 'Fixing bugs'
4. 'Minor changes'
5. 'Pls work'
6. 'I hate my life'

Senior: Code test kar liya?
Junior: Haan sir, compile ho gaya, matlab chal hi jayega.
Compiler: *Laughs in runtime exception*
Baki sab thik hai par ye hardware engineers ko kya lagta hai ki software hardware ko automatically control kar lega bina drivers ke?

Internet Explorer: Guys, 2026 me hum viral ho rahe hain na?
Chrome: Bro, tumhara update kab ka band ho gaya hai.

Ek programmer ne doosre se poocha: Tumhaari girlfriend kaisi hai?
Doosra: Null pointer exception, access nahi kar paa raha.

Client: Mujhe simple app chahiye, bas WhatsApp jaisa interface ho aur Amazon jaisa checkout.
Developer: Budget?
Client: Rs 5000 + ek achi rating.

Developer 1: Chal chai peete hain.
Developer 2: Ruko yaar, bas code deploy ho raha hai.
*Never seen Developer 2 again*
AI will replace coders. Haan bilkul! Kyunki client ko requirements explain karna khud ek logical process hai jo unhe kabhi nahi aayega.

When the code runs on the first try:
Developer: Mujhe thoda darr lag raha hai... isme koi chupa hua bug zaroor hai.

Boss: Aaj office late kyun aaye?
Employee: Sir, aapne hi toh kaha tha office ko apna ghar samjho. Toh thoda aaram se utha.

HR: Aapke resume me likha hai ki aap pressure handle kar sakte hain. Ek example dijiye?
Candidate: Sir, maine pure group project me akela kaam kiya tha.
Monday ko office jana is like: 'Chalo, dekh hi lete hain aaj kya naya kalesh hone wala hai.'

Boss: Mujhe ye report kal subah tak chahiye.
Me: Sir, main ek insaan hoon, machine nahi.
Boss: Acha, toh salary cash me chahiye ya machine-made bank transfer se?
Me: Report typing started sir.

Appraisal meeting ke baad:
Manager: Tumhara performance average raha.
Employee: Aur meri salary growth?
Manager: Wo toh bilkul non-existent rahi, standard format.

Office email rules:
'As per my last email' = Tum anpadh ho kya?
'Thanks in advance' = Tumhe ye karna hi padega, option nahi hai.

WFH ke dauran:
Meeting in progress:
Manager: Screen share karo.
Me: *Panics and closes 50 non-work tabs of shopping and memes*

Boss: Teamwork se bada kuch nahi hota.
Also Boss: Individual bonuses for the top performer only!
Work-life balance kya hota hai? Subah office ki tension, raat ko office ki dreams. Bas yahi hai balance.

HR: What is your salary expectation?
Candidate: I want enough to pay rent, eat 3 meals, and buy 1 cup of gourmet coffee without crying.
Corporate life: Jahan log Friday raat ko party karte hain aur Saturday-Sunday sirf ye sochte hain ki kal Monday hai.

Boss: Hum ek family hain.
Employee: Sir, fir mujhe monthly allowance do pocket money ke roop me aur kaam mat karao.

Me: Sir, main beemar hoon, aaj leave chahiye.
Manager: Acha, meeting me bol paoge?
Me: *Coughs dramatically* Sir, type kar paunga bas.
Office me jab lunch break hota hai, tab sab log aise khush hote hain jaise unhe jail se 1 ghante ki parole mili ho.

Boss to Intern: Beta, tumhare paas growth ke bahut options hain.
Intern: Sir, pehle stipend badha do, growth main khud dekh lungi.
Meeting that could have been an email: Day 485 of wondering why I am sitting in a room looking at a PowerPoint presentation on 'Strategic synergy'.
Corporate standard line: 'Let's take this offline.' = Abhi mere paas is faltu baat ka koi jawab nahi hai.

Desk job worker: Mera back pain badh gaya hai.
Doctor: Gym jao.
Desk job worker: Main gym me bhi baithne wali cycle chala sakta hoon kya?

Manager: Is project ke complete hone pe hum party karenge.
*Project completes in 2028*
Appraisal cycle is like: HR wants you to show innovation, Manager wants you to show compliance, and you just want to show up on time.

College Rules:
1. Subah 8 baje ki class me koi nahi jata.
2. Exam se ek din pehle sab padhte hain.
3. Viva me jo confidence se jhoot bolta hai, wahi pass hota hai.

Professor: Tumne copy kiya hai?
Student: Nahi sir, wo hum dono ke brain me wireless connection chal raha tha exam hall me.

Syllabus for the exam:
Chapter 1 to 10.
What I read: Chapter 1 (page 1 only).
What came in exam: Chapter 11 (Out of syllabus).

College group projects:
1 insaan kaam karta hai.
2 insaan sirf dekhne aate hain.
1 insaan bas PPT me apna naam check karta hai.
1 insaan toh gayab hi rehta hai.

Engineering student life:
First Year: Main NASA jaunga!
Second Year: IT company to pakki hai.
Third Year: Kisi tarah backlog clear ho jaye.
Final Year: TCS ka mass recruitment kab hai?

Exam Hall me dost ke ishaare:
1 ungli = Question 1 ka answer bata de.
Sir hilana = Bhai kuch nahi aata, paper khaali chord raha hoon.

Professor: Viva me minimum passing marks mil jayenge agar sahi answer diya.
Student: Sir, option A ya B me se kuch ho sakta hai kya?
College life is that magical time when you are broke, tired, stressed, and somehow having the best time of your life.

Hostel Warden: Raat ko 10 baje ke baad koi bahar nahi dikhna chahiye.
Students: Hum toh vaise bhi subah 4 baje tak jaagte hain sir.

Teacher: Paper bahut easy tha.
Students: Haan ma'am, aapse answer sheet check karwani thi na, isliye easy laga.
Exam time calculation: 3 ghante ka paper, 2.5 ghante window ke bahar dekhna, aur 30 mins me speed-of-light se likhna.
College library: Ek aisi shaant jagah jahan log padhne nahi, sirf free AC aur phone scroll karne aate hain.
Backlog students be like: Hum har semester me double fees dete hain college ko, building humare dum pe hi toh khadi hai.

Student: Papa college trip pe 5000 Rs mang rahe hain.
Papa: Acha? Aur padhai ke liye jo fees di thi uske results kahan hain?

Placement Officer: Aapki key skills kya hain?
Student: Main bina soye 24 ghante series dekh sakta hoon aur group assignments copy kar sakta hoon.
Assignments writing logic: Copy first line from Wikipedia, change the second line slightly, and copy paste the rest directly.

Morning lecture at 8:00 AM:
Attendance: 5 students.
9:00 AM when lecture is ending: 60 students show up for proxy.
College canteen ka khana is like a mystery box. Har din pasta ka taste badal jata hai, aur paneer humesha hard rehta hai.
Semester exams be like: Hum jo book padh ke gaye the wo local autowala padh raha tha, humare questions to aakash-ganga se aaye the.
Exam rules for backbenchers: Answer sheet ke peeche 'Jai Mata Di' likhna aur pass hone ki dua karna.

Boyfriend: Tumne kya kaha? Dobara bolna.
Girlfriend: Maine kuch nahi kaha.
Boyfriend: Acha, fir thik hai, mujhe laga meri galti ho gayi.
Single life ke fayde: Aapko kisi ko 'Khana khaya?' baar baar nahi poochhna padta. Bas khud khao aur so jao.

Girlfriend: Tumhe mere naye haircut ke baare me kya lagta hai?
Boyfriend: Bahut sundar hai.
Girlfriend: Tumhe kaise pata? Maine toh abhi tak cut karwaya hi nahi!
A true relationship is when you can look at each other and laugh because you both know how crazy you are.

Husband: Aaj khane me kya hai?
Wife: Zeher!
Husband: Thik hai, tum khao, main bahar se paneer tikka manga leta hoon.

Boyfriend: Hum ghoomne chalein?
Girlfriend: Haan, par main taiyar hone me 5 minute lungi.
*3 hours later* ...
Single log Valentine's Day pe: Aaj toh Bajrang Dal join kar ke park me ghoomne ka sahi time hai.

Girlfriend: Agar main kho gayi toh kya karoge?
Boyfriend: Main seedha police station jaunga aur unhe bataunga ki shanti mil gayi.

Relationship status:
[ ] Single
[ ] In a relationship
[x] Busy explaining that I am not looking for anyone right now.

Wife: Shaadi se pehle tum mujhe kitne gift dete the, ab kyun nahi dete?
Husband: Beta, kabhi kisi ne machli pakadne ke baad chara dala hai?

Boyfriend: Baby, main tumhare bina jee nahi paunga.
Girlfriend: Thik hai, toh hum kal subah 5 baje jogging pe chalein?
Boyfriend: Maine thoda exaggerate kiya tha vaise.
Single life is like: You want a relationship but when you see couples fighting over 'Tumne reply 2 mins late kyun kiya', you feel like a free bird.

Wife: Tumhe yaad hai aaj kya hai?
Husband: *Panics* Haan, bilkul... Humari anniversary?
Wife: Nahi, aaj trash throw karne ka day hai.

Boyfriend: Tum mere dil ki dhadkan ho.
Girlfriend: Toh mujhe apna credit card password do.
Boyfriend: Dhadkan ruk gayi hai baby.

Husband: Main ghar ka raja hoon.
Wife: Haan, aur main is kingdom ki supreme commander. Command follow karo raja ji.

Single: Mera birthday aane wala hai, kya gift chahiye?
Inner Me: Ek toffee kharid lo aur khud ko bol do - Happy Birthday bro.

Girlfriend: Tum mere liye tare tod sakte ho?
Boyfriend: Pehle tum terrace pe aana seekh lo bina seedhiyo ke, fir sochte hain.

Wife: Main thik lag rahi hoon?
Husband: Haan, bahut achi.
Wife: Tumne dhang se dekha bhi nahi!
Husband: *Looks for 10 seconds* Haan, ab thik hai.

Boyfriend: Tumhe mujhme kya sabse accha lagta hai?
Girlfriend: Tumhari ye galat-fehmiyan paalne ki aadat.
Single log jab couple ko public me dekhte hain: 'Inhe ghar wale dhoond rahe honge, main report kar deta hoon.'

Me: Papa, main doctor banna chahta hoon.
Papa: Beta, pehle apna room saaf kar, tab tera patient zinda bachega.
Sharma ji ka beta is like the final boss of Indian parenting. Aap kuch bhi karo, usne pehle hi usse double score kar rakha hai.

Mummy: Khana khao.
Me: Mujhe bhookh nahi hai.
Mummy: Toh thoda chawal khao aur side me sabzi bhi, bhookh apne aap lag jayegi.

Papa: Beta, mobile chalao aur chalate raho, din bhar scroll karo.
Me: *Suspicious* Papa, real me bol rahe ho?
Papa: Haan, aankhein kharab ho jayengi toh repair main nahi karaunga na.
Why did the tomato turn red? Kyunki usne dekha ki baaki saari veggies fridge me aaram se baithi hain aur wo salat me ja raha hai.

Desi Parents: Humare zamane me hum 10 km paidal chal ke school jaate the.
Me: Aur hum school bus chhootne pe direct chhutti le lete hain.

Mummy: Ye glass kisne toda?
Me: Hawa se toot gaya mumma.
Mummy: Hawa ka naam batao, abhi usko batati hoon chappal se.

Sharma Ji: Beta engineering ke baad kya plan hai?
Me: Sir, wahi jo engineers karte hain - MBA ya government job ki taiyari.
Why do we say 'Chai peeyo aur chill karo'? Kyunki garmi me garam chai peene se temperature balance ho jata hai, desi science!

Papa: Aaj kal ke bacche bahut sensitive hain.
Me: Papa, aapne hi toh bola tha ki daantne se darr lagna chahiye.
What do you call a sleeping fit person? Gym-nastic-nap.

Indian Relatives: Beta placements ho gayi? Package kitna hai?
Me: Sir, package toh thik hai, par privacy thoda kam hai.

Mummy: Beta, kitchen se zara dhaniya le aana.
Me: *Brings Pudina*
Mummy: Aaj se tumhara khana band.
Why do Indian fathers love newspapers? Kyunki unhe screen-time limits se bachna hota hai aur gyan baki sab ko dena hota hai.

Papa: Humare time me hum aam ki guthli se khelte the.
Me: Papa, tabhi toh aapne hume mobile dilaya, tarakki!

Guest: Beta kya kar rahe ho?
Me: Sir, engineering kar raha hoon.
Guest: Acha, fir toh ghar ke pankhe theek kar lete hoge?

Mummy: Shaadi kab karoge?
Me: Jab koi milegi.
Mummy: Hum dhoondenge toh 100% milegi. Tum bas haan bolna seekh lo.
Why do autowalas always say 'No'? Kyunki unka real passion rejected status check karna hai passenger ka face dekh kar.

Papa: Humare zamane me hum 10 Rs me poora mahina chalate the.
Me: Papa, tab petrol 2 Rs litre tha na, balance sheet thik karo.
What is the favorite game of a desi dad? 'Turn off the lights in the empty room' - Level 99 master.
Subah ka alarm bajte hi ek hi thought aata hai: 'Kya sach me padhai ya job itni zaroori hai? Kya main sanyas nahi le sakta?'
Indian roads ka golden rule: Agar signal green hai toh thoda sambhal ke chalo, red waale abhi bhi speed badha rahe hote hain.
Ghar me jab guest aate hain, toh unhe dry fruits milte hain. Aur unke jaane ke baad hume milta hai unka leftover kaju-katli.

Gym join karne ke 3 stages:
Day 1: Aaj se diet shuru, workout extreme.
Day 2: Body pain extreme, protein shake search.
Day 3: Pizza and chill, gym next month pakka.
Online shopping logic: Rs 2000 ka item cart me add kar liya. Par Rs 40 delivery fee dekh ke order cancel kar diya.
Ghar me purani plastic ki bottle ka real destiny: Fridge me thanda paani store karna tab tak jab tak wo toot na jaye.
Indian traffic me horn bajana is like sending a warning message: 'Main aa raha hoon, tum hato ya na hato, meri car nahi rukegi.'

Haircut ke baad barbour se dialogue: 'Bhaiya thoda zyaada chota kar diya.'
Barbour: 'Nahi bhaiya, 4 din me set ho jayega.'
Group trip planning: 10 dost haan bolenge, 5 dost budget puchenge, 2 dost cancel karenge, aur end me bas 2 log local tea stall pe milenge.
Dieting: Ek aisi activity jahan aap salad khate hain aur din bhar pizza ke photos scroll karte hain.
Ghar ka geyser aur bank balance ek jaise hain, jab garam paani ki zaroorat hoti hai tabhi dono response dena band kar dete hain.

Tailor: Bhaiya, shaadi kab hai?
Me: Agle mahine.
Tailor: Toh kapde uske agle mahine milenge, adjust kar lena.
Indian street food vendor making cheese sandwich: *Grates 5 blocks of cheese* 'Bhaiya, healthy hai, fat-free cheese hai.'
Mobile charging status: 99% pe battery 2 ghante chalegi, par 1% pe it turns into a superhero and survives for 30 minutes.

Me: Aaj main time pe sounga.
YouTube at 3:00 AM: 'How to build a swimming pool in the middle of a jungle using a spoon.'
Me: Extremely interesting, must watch.
Indian moms when they can't find something: 'Agar mujhe kitchen me mil gaya na toh chappal se pitungi.' *Finds it in 2 seconds*

Dentist: Aap teeth clean karne ke baad brush karte ho?
Me: Sir, brush karne ke baad hi toh clean hote hain, workflow reversed hai kya?
Winter morning schedule: Alarm bajega at 7:00 AM, blanket bolta hai '5 mins aur', and directly 9:30 AM pe aankh khulti hai.

Bhaiya to Auto driver: Bhaiya, metro station chaloge?
Auto driver: *Stares in absolute silence, accelerates and drives away*

Me to My wallet: 'Bhai, is month thoda sath de de.'
Wallet: 'Main toh khud empty internal storage ka error de raha hoon.'

Sharma ji: Beta placements ho gayi?
Me: Uncle, aapki beti ki shadi ho gayi?
Sharma ji: *Topic changed to weather*
Vegetable shopping with mom: 'Bhaiya, thoda dhaniya free me daal do, warna ghar me kalesh ho jayega.'

Electricity goes out for 2 seconds.
Every Indian neighborhood: *Everyone walks out of their balcony to check if neighbors also lost power*

Me: Doctor, mujhe neend nahi aati.
Doctor: Phone side me rakh do.
Me: Kuch aur medicine batao sir, ye toh impossible treatment hai.

Uber driver: Bhaiya location pe kaise aana hai?
Me: Bhaiya road seedhi hai, bas aage aake left le lena.
Driver: *Takes right and cancels ride*
Indian weddings: Jahan aadhi janta sirf paneer tikka aur gulab jamun ki line me khadi rehne aati hai, bride-groom se kisi ko matlab nahi.

Me looking at my old photos: 'Bhai, main tab kitna patla aur smart lagta tha.'
Also me back then: 'Main kitna mota aur ajeeb lagta hoon.'

Zomato delivery executive: Bhaiya gate pe khada hoon.
Me running to gate like my life depends on it because food is love.
Password requirements: 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 number, 1 special character, 1 secret ingredient, and your first pet's blood group.
Ghar me sabse bada jhoot: 'Bas 5 min me pahunch raha hoon.' - When you are actually still in bed wearing pajamas.

When you try to sleep early:
Brain: 'Do you remember that awkward thing you said to your class teacher in 3rd grade?'
Me: *Jaagta hoon ab*
Indian households: Safai sirf Diwali me hoti hai, baaki din toh dust ko bas adjust kiya jata hai.
Me trying to calculate math in supermarket: 'Rs 150 ka 10%... ruko, calculator nikalne do, dimag crash ho jayega.'
Why do we keep the plastic cover on the new TV remote? Kyunki safety is first priority, remote bhale hi 20 saal chale.

Me: Aaj main bilkul fresh feel kar raha hoon.
Alarm: *Rings for Monday morning*
Me: My disappointment is immeasurable.
Indian moms to courier guy: 'Bhaiya, packet me kya hai? Pura khol ke dikhao, humne toh bas chawal mangaya tha.'
Water bottle refilling duty in summer: Jo bottle khali chordega, wo pure parivar ka dushman ghoshit ho jata hai.
Me at a clothing store: 'Bhaiya, aisi dress dikhao jo party me bhi chale aur office me bhi aur sote waqt bhi comfortable ho.'

Life before mobile: Log newspaper padhte the.
Life after mobile: Log bathroom me mobile scroll karte hain jab tak pair so na jaye.
Desi parent logic: Agar beemar ho, toh mobile ki galti hai. Agar exam me fail hue, toh mobile ki galti hai.

Indian traffic inspector: Helmet kyun nahi pehna?
Me: Sir, ghar paas me hi hai, 2 minute ki doori hai.
Inspector: Fine toh 2 second me cut ho jayega beta.

Me: *Sees a cute dog*
Also me: *Speaks in high-pitched baby voice* 'Who's a good boy?'
Dog: *Glares in judgement*
College canteen tea: Chai me doodh aur patti toh thik hai, par ye paani ka level ocean jaisa kyun lag raha hai?
Supermarket shopping: Wants to buy bread. Walks out with bread, 3 packs of chips, chocolates, a new mug, and no money left.
Indian household medicine cabinet: Combiflam for headache, Combiflam for leg pain, Combiflam for everything.

Me: Aaj main 8 ghante self-study karunga.
My Brain: 'Let's check if the ceiling fan is rotating at 100 RPM or 120 RPM.'

Tailor to candidate: 'Bhaiya, fit toh thik hai par thoda dhila lag raha hai.'
Candidate: 'Maine weight lose kiya hai abhi.'
Tailor: 'Toh kapda loose ho gaya automatic?'
Indian family Whatsapp groups: 90% 'Good Morning' rose images, 9% fake news about UNESCO declaring national anthem best, 1% real family info.
Me trying to click 'Skip Ad' on YouTube like it's a quick-time event in an action game.
Desi parent compliment: 'Aaj kaam toh thik kiya hai, par daily aisa karega toh hi maane.'
Morning walking routine: Walks for 10 minutes, eats 4 samosas, drinks 1 cup sweet tea. Fitness goals achieved!

Me on social media: *Posting cool vacation pics*
Me in reality: *Sitting on bed with messy hair eating maggi*
Indian train journey: The ultimate social experience where you know the entire life story of the passenger sitting next to you within 30 minutes.
Online class during corona: Camera off, Mic off, Pillow on, Blankets on. Best educational experience.
Indian tea logic: Monsoon me tea, summer me tea, winter me tea, headache me tea, breakup me tea, tea is the solution to everything.

Doctor: Gym jao aur walk karo.
Me: Doctor, walk toh main kitchen tak kar hi leta hoon paneer nikalne ke liye.

Mom: 'Phone rakh de, aankhein lal ho gayi hain.'
Me: 'Mom, ye screen filter ki wajah se hai, look blue filter on.'

Courier guy: Bhaiya signature kar do.
Me: *Draws a random wave that looks nothing like my real signature*
Me looking at my bank balance at the end of the month: 'Who hacked my account and spent Rs 45 on pani puri? Oh, wait, it was me.'
Indian mothers cooking logic: A pinch of salt, a pinch of turmeric, and a gallon of mother's love (and occasional flying chappal warning).
Me trying to find the end of the cellotape: *Scratching the roll for 10 minutes in frustration*

Barbour: Bhaiya, facial karwa lo, glow aayega.
Me: Bhai, is chehre pe glow sirf filter se aa sakta hai, product se nahi.

Uber Auto: 'Ride starting OTP?'
Me: '1234.'
Driver: 'Acha, mera password bhi yahi hai.'
Me at a restaurant: Ordering water bottle instead of regular water because hygiene is important. Also me: Eats street side golgappa with direct tap water.
Indian dads when they watch news: *Sitting with folded arms, shaking head, giving geopolitical advice directly to the TV screen*
My 5-year-old laptop when I open Chrome with 3 tabs: *Fans spinning at speed of jet engine, ready to take off*
Shopping with dad: 'Ye kya 2000 ki t-shirt? Humare zamane me isme poora thaan milta tha.'
My bed is a magical place where I suddenly remember everything I forgot to do today, including breathing.
Indian homes: A soap bar is used until it becomes a thin paper slice, then it is stuck onto the new soap bar. Peak efficiency!

Me at the dentist: *Has 5 tubes and 2 hands in mouth*
Dentist: 'Toh beta engineering kaisi chal rahi hai?'
Me: 'Mmmph ghhff.'

Weekend plans: 'I will clean the house, read a book, and go for run.'
Actual weekend: *Scrolls Instagram for 14 hours in bed*
Indian traffic: Green means go, Yellow means speed up to clear, and Red means 5 more cars can cross quickly.

Me trying to show a meme to my mom: *Explaining the entire backstory and internet culture for 20 mins*
Mom: 'Acha, ye kaun hai?'
Going to a wedding just to check out the food menu and rate the paneer quality: A national hobby.
Indian mothers to their kids at 12 PM: 'Sote raho din bhar, dopahar ho gayi hai.' (Actual time: 9:00 AM).

Me: *Speaks English with a fancy accent once*
My Friends: 'Bhai, London se kab lauta?'
Vegetable shopping: 'Bhaiya aaloo thoda bada dena, small waale peel karne me aafat aati hai.'
My email inbox: 999+ unread notifications from random websites I visited once in 2018.
Me at the gym: *Lifts 5kg dumbbell once* *Looks in the mirror for biceps* 'Growth toh dikh rahi hai.'
Indian dads fixing anything: 'Ek screwdriver lana aur cellotape.' 10 mins later: The fan is fixed (temporarily with tape).
Zomato delivery track: 'Raju is carrying your order with care.' Me looking out of window like a waiting lover.
Indian student calculating marks: 'Agar steps ke marks mil gaye aur examiner khush raha toh 33 pakke hain.'
Me trying to wake up early: Sets alarms at 6:00, 6:05, 6:10, 6:15. *Wakes up at 8:30 and deletes all alarms*.
Indian street food vendor: 'Bhaiya, teekha thik hai na?' Me: *Crying, sweat dripping, tongue burning* 'Haan bhaiya, bilkul mast.'
My room is not dirty, it's just decorated in 'convenience-first' style. Everything is on the floor.
Mom: 'Aata gundh de.' Me: *Brings scientific scale to measure water to flour ratio so it doesn't become liquid soup*.
Indian relative: 'Beta shadi kab kar rahe ho?' Me: 'Aap shadi me kab tak aayenge, ticket book karwani hai?' Relative: *Silent*
Me: *Tries to cook Maggi in 2 minutes* Zomato delivery: 30 minutes. My patience: 0. Maggi cooked in 15 mins because physics exists.
Indian traffic rule: If a cow is standing in the middle of road, it has right of way. No horn, just wait and pray.
Me when I open my wardrobe: 50 t-shirts inside. 'Bhai, pehenne ke liye kuch nahi hai, shopping karni padegi.'
Indian moms: Can find anything in the house, but if they lose their glasses which are on their head, the house is in red alert.
Group trip discussion: Starts in WhatsApp, ends in archived chats. Legend says the trip is still being planned.
When you pay with Rs 2000 note for a Rs 10 item, the shopkeeper looks at you like you are trying to buy his shop.
Me at a clothing store: 'Bhaiya, discount kitna hai?' Shopkeeper: 'Sir fix rate hai.' Me: *Walks away* Shopkeeper: 'Bhaiya ruko, 10% de dunga.'
My bank balance statement is just a list of UPI transfers of Rs 10, Rs 20, Rs 50 for chai and samosa.
Indian moms during summer: 'Fan off karo, AC chal raha hai.' Also moms: 'AC band karo, bill zyaada aa raha hai.'
Me trying to open a plastic bag at the supermarket: *Licking my finger to get grip, failing, feeling judged by people behind*.
Indian dad: 'Beta, results kya aaye?' Me: 'Papa, topper ke 95 aaye hain.' Dad: 'Aur tumhare?' Me: 'Papa topper ke 95 aaye hain, context dekho.'
My bed when I have to wake up: Feels like a luxury 7-star resort. My bed when I want to sleep: Feels like a bag of potatoes.
Indian roads rule: Indicator right ka chal raha hai toh gaadi left zaroor mudegi, keep safe distance.

A SQL query goes into a restaurant, walks up to two tables and asks, 'Can I join you?'
The waiter says, 'Sure, but only if you have a common key!'

Developer: I have resolved the merge conflicts!
Git: That's cute, now resolve the 45 logic errors you just introduced.
Programming is like writing a book... except if you miss a single comma on page 147, the whole book makes no sense.

Why do programmers always mix up Halloween and Christmas?
Because Oct 31 == Dec 25!
There are two ways to write error-free programs; only the third one works.

Developer: Hamara server down ho gaya!
Client: Restart karke dekha?
Developer: Sir, ye system nahi, datacenter me aag lag gayi hai.

What is the difference between a bug and a feature?
A bug is a mistake in logic. A feature is a mistake that looks intentional and is documented.

Junior: Sir, code optimization chal raha hai.
Senior: Tumne toh 80% comments hi delete kar diye!
Junior: Code size chota ho gaya na sir!
Why did the developer go broke? Because he used up all his cache!
A user interface is like a joke. If you have to explain it, it's not that good.

Developer's equation:
No Coffee = No Code
Code = Bugs
Therefore, Coffee = Bugs. Math never lies!

Keyboard shortcut for a developer's real workflow:
Ctrl+C -> Ctrl+V -> Pray -> Cry -> Ctrl+Z.
If at first you don't succeed, call it version 1.0 and release it anyway.
Why did the functions stop talking to each other? Because they had no arguments.

Compiler: Line 45 has a syntax error.
Me: *Fixes line 45*
Compiler: 147 new errors found. Have a nice day!
My keyboard is missing the Ctrl, Alt, and Delete keys. I guess my PC is completely out of control.

Developer: Sir, database lock ho gaya.
Admin: Kisne kiya?
Developer: Pata nahi sir, select queries chala rahe the, automatically lock ho gaya.
Software developers are the only professionals who get paid to solve problems they created themselves.

C++ programmer's marriage invitation:
'Welcome to the Constructor of our lives. No memory leaks allowed.'
Why did the JavaScript developer wear glasses? Because he couldn't C#.
Why did the bicycle collapse? Because it was two-tired!
What do you call a fake noodle? An impasta!
Why can't a nose be 12 inches long? Because then it would be a foot!
Why did the scarecrow win an award? Because he was outstanding in his field.
What do you call a snowman with a temper tantrum? A meltdown.

Mummy: Beta, jhaadoo laga do.
Me: Mumma, main thak gaya hoon.
Mummy: Toh thoda chappal ka taste karoge? Energy turant aa jayegi.

I told my doctor that I only sleep 3 hours a day. He asked, 'Why?'
I said, 'Because I sleep 8 hours at night!'
Why did the student bring a ladder to school? Because they wanted to go to high school!
What did the zero say to the eight? Nice belt!
Indian dad: Beta, 10th me top kar lo, life set hai. 12th me top kar lo, life set hai. Engineering me top kar lo... *To be continued*
Why did the banana go to the doctor? Because it wasn't peeling well!
What do you call a fish with no eyes? A fsh!
Indian mom logic: Agar mobile silent pe hai toh phone hi kyun rakha hai? Bech do isko kabaadi wale ko!
How do you organize a space party? You planet!
Why don't skeletons fight each other? They don't have the guts.
Desi dad looking at AC temperature: '24 pe kyun rakha hai? 26 karo isko, bill tumhaare papa bharenge?'
What do you call an alligator in a vest? An investigator.
Why did the math book look sad? Because it had too many problems.
Indian relatives checking your height: 'Beta tum toh bade ho gaye, aashirwad humara kaam kar gaya.'
What did the ocean say to the shore? Nothing, it just waved.

HR: What is your biggest weakness?
Me: I am too honest.
HR: I don't think that's a weakness.
Me: I don't care what you think.

My daily routine at work:
9:00 AM - Arrive.
9:15 AM - Coffee.
9:30 AM to 5:00 PM - Pretend to look busy while scrolling articles.
5:30 PM - Exit.

Manager: Humare paas 24/7 client support hai.
Developer: Matlab humein raat ko bhi call aa sakti hai?
Manager: Haan, rotation basis pe, which means you.
Nothing says 'I trust my employees' like requiring a 3-page doctor's note for a single day of sick leave.
Corporate life: Where you spend 2 hours in a meeting discussing how to save 10 minutes on a daily report.

Me: Sir, salary increment kab hoga?
Boss: Beta, inflation high hai, budget constraint hai.
Also Boss: *Arrives at office in a brand new luxury SUV*
Job description: Entry-level position. Requires 5 years of experience, a master's degree, and 3 recommendation letters from NASA.

Manager: I need this updated immediately.
Me: Sent it to you 3 hours ago.
Manager: Oh, let me search my inbox. Ah, yes, here it is.
Working from home is great. I can roll out of bed at 8:58 AM, open my laptop at 9:00 AM, and start making coffee in my pajamas.
Office gossip is the only database in the company that has 100% uptime and updates in real-time.

Manager: We need to increase our synergy.
Employee: Synergy ka matlab kya hota hai sir?
Manager: Synergy means working together synergistically!
I have a lot of jokes about unemployed people, but none of them work.

HR email at 4:55 PM on Friday: 'Urgent policy update regarding office dress code.'
Everyone: *Panics and closes work*

Me: I need a mental health day off.
Manager: Sure, just complete all your deliverables by Thursday midnight.
My desk is like a museum of coffee cups from different days of the week.

Intern: Sir, is company me exit policy kya hai?
HR: 3 months notice period or your soul, whichever comes first.

Boss: Why are you not working?
Employee: Because I didn't see you coming.

Corporate slogan: 'Think outside the box.'
Also Corporate: 'Please follow the standard templates strictly without any deviation.'
Every Friday afternoon, my motivation level drops faster than the server speed during a mass deployment.

Manager: Let's do a quick alignment call.
*Alignment call lasts for 1.5 hours and finishes with zero alignment*

Viva Examiner: CPU ka full form kya hai?
Student: Control Power Unit?
Examiner: Beta, Computer Science drop karke cooking class join kar lo.

Student during exam: *Staring at the ceiling*
Invigilator: Kuch likho beta.
Student: Sir, ceiling decoration bahut clean hai, focus kar raha hoon.
College: A place where you pay thousands of dollars to teach yourself everything using YouTube videos the night before the exam.
Backbencher logic: Agar frontbencher answer chhupa raha hai, toh use question sheet ke answers galat batana start kar do.

Professor: Turn off your phones.
Student: Sir, phone generator silent calculator mode pe hai.

Viva Examiner: Binary code kya hota hai?
Student: Zero aur One sir.
Examiner: Aur kuch?
Student: Zero, one, zero, one, zero...

My parents: Engineering degree se life safe ho jayegi.
Me after graduation: Free placement preparation guide search kar raha hoon.

Professor: Attendance is mandatory. 75% required to sit for exams.
Me: *Calculates minimum number of classes I can bunk without getting debarred*
Viva prep stage: Read the index of the book, look at the diagrams, and try to sound extremely confident while saying 'I don't recall this sir'.
Engineering labs are places where you connect wire A to socket B, and hope nothing catches fire or explodes.

Student: Sir, paper difficult tha.
Teacher: Book ke andar se hi tha.
Student: Sir, book toh library me locked thi na!
College canteen samosa: A historic item that has more air and potato peelings than actual potato fill.
Backbencher during class presentation: *Nods confidently at every slide without understanding a single word*
Class WhatsApp group before exam: 'Bhai, notes send kar de.' 'Pls copy pdf send kar.' 'Anyone has unit 3 notes?'
My GPA is like a roller coaster. Except it only goes down, there are no upward loops.

Professor: Why is your assignment identical to your friend's?
Student: Because we both worked together, joint venture sir.
Student's logic during multiple-choice questions: 'Option C is looking very professional, let's select C.'
College life is when you wake up at 11:00 AM, feel tired, take a nap at 2:00 PM, and sleep at 4:00 AM.

Professor: Any doubts?
*Dead silence*
Students in exam: 'Ye question kab padhaya tha class me?'
Backbenchers in lab: 'Bhai, output display ho gaya toh copy paste kar dena, main screen black-out handle karunga.'

Girlfriend: What are you thinking about?
Boyfriend: Nothing.
Girlfriend: But you looked so sad.
Boyfriend: I was thinking about why the dinosaur went extinct.
Single life status: My phone battery lasts for 2 days because nobody is sending me 'Baby, are you angry?' texts.

Husband: Wife control panel kahan hai?
System: Error 404, file not found. System is controlled by Wife default settings.

Girlfriend: I want you to be more romantic.
Boyfriend: *Brings a bouquet of red roses and a box of chocolates*
Girlfriend: Tumhe ye ideas kisne diye? Kiska affair chal raha hai?

Wife: Tumhe meri yaad nahi aati?
Husband: Bilkul aati hai, tabhi toh main der se ghar aata hoon taaki surprise bana rahe.

Boyfriend: Baby, main tumhare liye chaand-taare tod sakta hoon.
Girlfriend: Mujhe naya iPhone chahiye.
Boyfriend: Chaand hi thik hai, aashirwad mil jayega free me.
Relationship rule: When your partner says 'Do whatever you want', DO NOT DO WHAT YOU WANT. It is a trap!

Wife: Meraj kaisa lag raha hoon?
Husband: Acha.
Wife: Acha matlab normal? Sundar nahi lag rahi?
Husband: *Sighs and logs off life*
My girlfriend is like square root of -1. A complete mathematical imagination.

Husband: Shaadi ke baad shanti mili.
Friend: Really?
Husband: Haan, shanti devi meri padosi hain, unke yahan noise kam hota hai.

Boyfriend: What do you want to eat?
Girlfriend: Anything.
Boyfriend: Pizza?
Girlfriend: No, too oily.
Boyfriend: Sushi?
Girlfriend: No, raw fish.
Boyfriend: Burgers?
Girlfriend: No, too heavy.
Boyfriend: What then?
Girlfriend: Anything!
Single life is when your bed has 5 pillows and you use all of them to create a comfortable fort for yourself.

Wife: Tumhe mera kaunsa birthday yaad hai?
Husband: Wo jab tumne mujhe expensive watch mangi thi aur maine credit card limit dikhayi thi.

Boyfriend: Baby, your eyes are like stars.
Girlfriend: Acha? Aur mera face?
Boyfriend: Facetime call check karo, internet issues hain lagta hai.
Marriage is a workshop where the husband works and the wife shops. Perfect balance of trade.

Single: I am waiting for my soulmate.
Universe: Keep waiting, your soulmate is currently busy watching memes on their phone.

Girlfriend: I look fat in this dress?
Boyfriend: No, you look amazing.
Girlfriend: You are lying just to make me happy!
Boyfriend: *Error, system shutdown*

Wife: Hum ghoomne kab jayenge?
Husband: Kal subah.
*Next morning: Rain starts*
Husband: Climate support nahi kar raha, ghar pe hi paneer pakode banate hain.

Boyfriend: Baby, your voice is music to my ears.
Girlfriend: *Starts talking about her shopping list*
Boyfriend: Please mute the music for 5 minutes.

Single log: Hum single hi acche hain, no drama, no expense.
Also single log: *Sees a cute couple* 'Hey Bhagwan, mera number kab aayega?'
My phone fell face down on the floor. It was the longest 3 seconds of my life waiting to flip it over.

Zomato track: 'Delivery partner has reached your building.'
Me standing near the elevator waiting to grab the food like a hungry tiger.
Indian household rule: Naye kapde normal use me aate hain, fir night dress bante hain, fir dusting cloth (ponchha) ban jate hain.
Me looking at my UPI statement: 'Rs 10, Rs 20, Rs 15... Chai aur momos ne milkar mera bank balance crash kar diya.'
I need a 6-month vacation twice a year to recover from my daily screen-scrolling fatigue.
Vegetable shopping: 'Bhaiya green chillies thodi extra daal do na, mumma ne bola tha free me milti hain.'

Electricity cut in summer:
Step 1: Check if neighbors have power.
Step 2: Neighbors also don't have power -> Immediate relief and satisfaction.

Tailor: Bhaiya, fitting tight karni hai ya loose?
Me: Bhaiya, aisi karo ki lunch ke baad button na tootey.

Me: Today I will eat healthy salad.
*Opens food app*
App: 60% discount on Butter Chicken and Garlic Naan.
Me: Health can wait, discount can't.
Nothing ruins a Sunday afternoon like the realization that tomorrow is Monday morning.
Why do we keep empty boxes of expensive items in our closets? 'Ghar me decoration ke liye kaam aayega' - Desi logic!

Me trying to explain my career to my grandmother: 'Dadi, main computer me code likhta hoon.'
Dadi: 'Toh computer repair ki shop khol le, acha scope hai.'
Water bottle duty in summer: If you drink the last drop and put the empty bottle back in the fridge, you are a criminal.

Zomato review: 'Food was excellent but delivery guy did not smile. 4 stars.'
Delivery guy: 'Bhaiya 45 degree garmi me cycle chala raha tha.'

Me: I need to save money.
Also me: 'Oh, this Rs 2000 keyboard has RGB lights! Added to cart.'
Indian moms can find your lost socks in a room that you have searched 5 times, using their magical navigation sensors.
When you try to use a public restroom and the lock is broken: *Holding the door handle with the strength of 100 soldiers*

Uber driver: 'Bhaiya right side me aao.'
Me: 'Sir, yahan toh divider hai, kaise aau?'
Driver: 'Jump karke aao auto khadi hai.'
My bed has a gravity pull that is 10 times stronger than the earth's gravity, especially on Monday mornings.
Indian tea logic: Monsoon me tea, summer me tea, winter me tea, headache me tea, breakup me tea, tea is the solution to everything.
I have 50 items in my shopping cart, waiting for a salary credit that is already spent in my mind.

Shopkeeper: 'Bhaiya change nahi hai, Rs 5 ki toffee le lo.'
Me: 'Acha, toh kal main toffee dekar bread le sakta hoon?'

Me: Today I will go to bed early.
My brain at 2:00 AM: 'How are marbles made in factories? Let's watch a 40-minute documentary.'
Why do Indian families use old cookie boxes to store sewing kits (sui-dhaaga)? It is a national trap for hungry kids.
Me at a clothing store: 'Bhaiya, aisi dress dikhao jo party me bhi chale aur office me bhi aur sote waqt bhi comfortable ho.'

Dentist: Teeth clean karne ke baad brush karte ho?
Me: Sir, brush karne ke baad hi toh clean hote hain, workflow reversed hai kya?
Indian dad fixing anything: 'Ek screwdriver lana aur black tape.' 10 mins later: The fan is fixed (temporarily with tape).
Zomato track: 'Raju is carrying your order with care.' Me looking out of window like a waiting lover.
Indian student calculating marks: 'Agar steps ke marks mil gaye aur examiner khush raha toh 33 pakke hain.'
Me trying to find the end of the cellotape: *Scratching the roll for 10 minutes in frustration*
Indian moms to their kids at 12 PM: 'Sote raho din bhar, dopahar ho gayi hai.' (Actual time: 9:00 AM).
Going to a wedding just to check out the food menu and rate the paneer quality: A national hobby.
My 5-year-old laptop when I open Chrome with 3 tabs: *Fans spinning at speed of jet engine, ready to take off*
Indian homes: A soap bar is used until it becomes a thin paper slice, then it is stuck onto the new soap bar. Peak efficiency!

Me: *Speaks English with a fancy accent once*
My Friends: 'Bhai, London se kab lauta?'

Weekend plans: 'I will clean the house, read a book, and go for run.'
Actual weekend: *Scrolls Instagram for 14 hours in bed*
Indian traffic: Green means go, Yellow means speed up to clear, and Red means 5 more cars can cross quickly.
My room is not dirty, it's just decorated in 'convenience-first' style. Everything is on the floor.
Indian relative: 'Beta shadi kab kar rahe ho?' Me: 'Aap shadi me kab tak aayenge, ticket book karwani hai?' Relative: *Silent*
Indian dads when they watch news: *Sitting with folded arms, shaking head, giving geopolitical advice directly to the TV screen*
My bank balance statement is just a list of UPI transfers of Rs 10, Rs 20, Rs 50 for chai and samosa.
Indian moms during summer: 'Fan off karo, AC chal raha hai.' Also moms: 'AC band karo, bill zyaada aa raha hai.'
Indian roads rule: Indicator right ka chal raha hai toh gaadi left zaroor mudegi, keep safe distance.
Why do we keep the plastic cover on the new TV remote? Kyunki safety is first priority, remote bhale hi 20 saal chale.
Zomato delivery track: 'Raju is carrying your order with care.' Me looking out of window like a waiting lover.
Indian street food vendor: 'Bhaiya, teekha thik hai na?' Me: *Crying, sweat dripping, tongue burning* 'Haan bhaiya, bilkul teekha.'
My bed when I have to wake up: Feels like a luxury 7-star resort. My bed when I want to sleep: Feels like a bag of potatoes.
Indian moms: Can find anything in the house, but if they lose their glasses which are on their head, the house is in red alert.
When you pay with Rs 2000 note for a Rs 10 item, the shopkeeper looks at you like you are trying to buy his shop.
Indian family Whatsapp groups: 90% 'Good Morning' rose images, 9% fake news, 1% real family info.
